content: To follow the path, look to the master, follow the master, walk with the master, see through the master, become the master.
locator: Traditional Zen proverb

title: When Walking, Just Walk
content: When walking, just walk. When eating, just eat.
locator: Traditional Zen proverb

title: Seek What They Sought
content: Do not seek to follow in the footsteps of the wise; seek what they sought.
locator: Traditional Zen proverb, after Matsuo Basho

title: Drink Up the Yangtze River
content:
A monk asked Mazu, "What is the meaning of Bodhidharma's coming from the West?"

Mazu said, "I'll tell you after you've drunk up the Yangtze River in one gulp."

The monk stood speechless.
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Mazu chapter

title: The Wild Fox Koan
content:
Every time Baizhang gave a talk, an old man would attend and listen. One day the old man stayed behind and said, "I am not a human being. In the time of Kashyapa Buddha I was the head monk on this mountain. A student asked me, 'Does a person of great cultivation fall under the law of cause and effect, or not?' I answered, 'Such a person does not fall under cause and effect.' For this I was reborn as a wild fox for five hundred lifetimes. I beg you, Master, give me a turning word so that I may be released."

Baizhang said, "Such a person does not evade cause and effect."

At these words the old man was greatly enlightened.
locator: Mumonkan, Case 2

title: The One Mind That All Buddhas Teach
content:
Huangbo said, "The dharma that all Buddhas teach is the same dharma—the One Mind. This Mind is without beginning, is unborn and indestructible. It is not green nor yellow, and has no form or appearance. It does not belong to the categories of things which exist or do not exist, nor is it to be considered new or old. It is neither long nor short, big nor small, for it transcends all limits, measures, names, traces, and comparisons."

A monk asked, "Then how should one practice?"

Huangbo said, "There is nothing to practice. Just let things be in their own way, and there will be neither coming nor going. This is called the One Mind, and there is no other dharma besides it."
locator: Chuanxin Fayao (Essential Dharma of Mind Transmission)

title: Being-Time (Uji)
content: An ancient Buddha said, "For the time being, I stand on the highest mountain peak. For the time being, I move along the deepest ocean floor." 'For the time being' here means time itself is being, and all being is time. Each moment is all being, is the entire world. Reflect now whether any being or any world is left out of the present moment. Being-time has the quality of flowing. Today flows into tomorrow, today flows into yesterday. This flowing does not mean that time moves from today to tomorrow. Today is the time of today; yesterday is the time of yesterday. They are not separate, and yet they do not merge.
locator: Shobogenzo, Uji (Being-Time)

title: Spring Does Not Become Summer
content: Firewood becomes ash, and does not become firewood again. Yet do not think that the ash is after and the firewood is before. Firewood abides in its dharma-position as firewood, with its own before and after. Spring does not become summer. We do not say that spring becomes summer. There is spring, and there is summer. Each abides in its own dharma-position completely. This is being-time. Life does not become death. Death does not become life. Life is its own complete moment; death is its own complete moment. They are like winter and spring—we do not say that winter becomes spring, or that spring becomes winter.
locator: Shobogenzo, Genjokoan

title: Does a Dog Have Buddha Nature?
content:
A monk asked Zhaozhou, "Does a dog have Buddha nature, or not?"

Zhaozhou said, "Wu!" [No!]

This single word—Mu—has become the first barrier that countless students of Zen have thrown themselves against. It is not an answer to be understood with the intellect. Zhaozhou's Mu is not the opposite of 'yes.' It cuts through the very question, through the one who asks, through all dualistic thinking. To penetrate Mu is to penetrate the gateless barrier itself.
locator: Zhaozhou Lu (Recorded Sayings of Zhaozhou)

title: I Have Been to the South and the North
content:
Zhaozhou went to a hermit's place and asked, "Are you in? Are you in?"

The hermit raised his fist.

Zhaozhou said, "The water is too shallow for a ship to anchor." He left.

He went to another hermit's place and asked, "Are you in? Are you in?"

The hermit also raised his fist.

Zhaozhou said, "Freely you give, freely you take away. Freely you bestow life, freely you destroy." He bowed deeply.

When asked why he approved of one and not the other when both raised their fists, Zhaozhou said, "I have been to the South and the North. I know the lay of the land."
locator: Zhaozhou Lu, the two hermits

title: Be the Master Wherever You Are
content: Be the master wherever you are, and wherever you stand is the true place. No matter what circumstances come your way, they cannot pull you off center. Even if you are confronted by the evil influences of old karma, or by the five kinds of uninterrupted hell, these become for you the great ocean of emancipation. Students today cannot get anywhere because they base their understanding on the interpretations of old masters. If you want to be free, be the master right now. Do not be swayed by anything. Break through every obstacle you meet, and the place where you stand will be genuine.
locator: Linji Lu (Record of Linji), Discourses

title: Zen Is Experience, Not Philosophy
content: Zen is not a philosophy. It is not a system of thought. It is the experience of living reality, here and now. You cannot capture it in concepts, though concepts may point toward it. You cannot find it in books, though books may inspire you to look. The experience of kensho is as definite and unmistakable as the experience of tasting water—you know immediately whether it is warm or cold. No one can tell you what water tastes like; you must drink it yourself.
locator: Zen: The Authentic Gate, on the nature of Zen

title: Not Mind, Not Buddha
content:
A monk asked Mazu, "What is Buddha?"

Mazu said, "This mind is Buddha."

Later, another monk asked, "What is Buddha?"

Mazu said, "Not mind, not Buddha."

When asked why he gave two different answers, Mazu said, "'This mind is Buddha' is to stop a baby from crying. Once the crying has stopped, I say 'Not mind, not Buddha.' The first is medicine for the sick; the second takes away the medicine when the sickness is cured."
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Mazu chapter

title: Body Exposed in the Golden Wind
content:
A monk asked Yunmen, "When the tree withers and the leaves fall, what then?"

Yunmen said, "Body exposed in the golden wind."
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Case 27

title: The Seamless Monument
content:
Dongshan was unwell. A monk asked, "Your Reverence is ill, but is there anyone who does not get ill?"

Dongshan said, "There is."

The monk asked, "Does the one who does not get ill look after Your Reverence?"

Dongshan said, "This old monk is properly looking after that one."

The monk asked, "How is it when you look after that one?"

Dongshan said, "Then I do not see that there is any illness."
locator: Recorded Sayings of Dongshan, on illness

title: A Flower in a Dream
content:
The official Lu Geng said to Nanquan, "The teaching of Sengzhao says, 'Heaven and earth and I share one root; the myriad things and I are of one body.' This is marvelous indeed!"

Nanquan pointed to a flower in the garden and said, "People of this age see this flower as if in a dream."
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Case 40

title: Nothing Within, Nothing Without
content:
Deshan came to the dharma hall and said, "Tonight I will not answer any questions. Anyone who asks a question gets thirty blows of the stick."

A monk came forward and bowed.

Deshan immediately struck him.

The monk said, "I haven't even asked a question yet. Why do you hit me?"

Deshan said, "Where are you from?"

The monk said, "From Silla."

Deshan said, "Before you even boarded the ship, you already deserved thirty blows."
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Deshan's thirty blows

title: Think Neither Good Nor Evil
content:
The monk Huiming pursued the Sixth Patriarch to seize the robe and bowl. When he caught up, Huineng placed the robe and bowl on a rock and said, "This robe represents the dharma. It should not be fought over by force. Take it if you wish."

Huiming tried to lift them but could not. Trembling, he said, "I came for the dharma, not for the robe."

Huineng said, "Think neither good nor evil. At this very moment, what is your original face—the face you had before your mother and father were born?"

At these words, Huiming was greatly enlightened.
locator: Platform Sutra, encounter with Huiming

title: The Meaning of Yangqi's Teaching
content:
A monk asked Yangqi, "What is the meaning of the patriarch's coming from the West?"

Yangqi said, "A donkey peers into a well."

The monk said, "I do not understand."

Yangqi said, "The well peers at the donkey."
locator: Wudeng Huiyuan, Yangqi Fanghui chapter

title: The Directness of Realization
content: Yangqi addressed the assembly: "If you want to understand this matter, you must cut off all entanglements at a single stroke. Do not spend your days counting other people's treasures. Right here, right now—what is lacking?"
locator: Wudeng Huiyuan, Yangqi Fanghui dharma hall discourse

title: The Essence of Chan
content:
A student asked Xutang, "What is the essence of Chan?"

Xutang said, "The cypress tree withers, the stone bridge flows."

The student said, "I don't grasp your meaning."

Xutang said, "The autumn wind has no fixed direction."
locator: Xutang Heshang Yulu, encounter dialogues

title: The Barrier of Words
content: Xutang said: "Words are a painted cake that cannot satisfy hunger, yet without words there is no way to point to the moon. The one who clings to words is lost; the one who abandons words is equally lost. Tell me—where do you stand?"
locator: Xutang Heshang Yulu, dharma hall discourse

title: The Great Doubt Block
content: Gaofeng said: "Beneath the great doubt there is great awakening. If you can raise the doubt mass to fill your entire body so that it becomes like a solid block—no gap, no crack, no place for thinking to enter—then suddenly it will shatter of itself. When this happens, you will be like a person who has been dead and comes back to life, or like a person who snatches back what has been stolen. Only then will you know that you yourself have always had it."
locator: Gaofeng Yuanmiao Chanshi Yulu, instruction on doubt

title: Xueyan Tests a Student
content:
A monk came to Xueyan and said, "I have come from a distant province specifically to receive your teaching."

Xueyan said, "Why did you leave where you were?"

The monk said, "To seek the Dharma."

Xueyan said, "The Dharma in your home province—was it deficient somehow?"

The monk was speechless.

Xueyan said, "You have traveled a thousand miles carrying a load that was never yours to begin with. Put it down."
locator: Wudeng Huiyuan, Xueyan Zuqin chapter

title: Not Deceived by Words
content: Baiyun said: "The ancients used words to dispel delusion, the way a physician prescribes medicine according to the illness. If you take the words of the ancients to be the truth itself, you are like a man who swallows the prescription instead of taking the medicine. Once the illness is cured, throw away the remedy. Do not let the teachings become a new disease."
locator: Wudeng Huiyuan, Baiyun Shouduan chapter

title: The Meaning of Practice
content:
A monk asked Wuzhun, "What is the meaning of a lifetime of practice?"

Wuzhun said, "Walking, standing, sitting, lying down."

The monk said, "Everyone walks, stands, sits, and lies down. How is that practice?"

Wuzhun said, "When I walk, I only walk. When I sit, I only sit. You walk and think of sitting. You sit and think of walking. That is the difference."
locator: Wuzhun Shifan Chanshi Yulu, encounter dialogues

title: The Blind Ass
content:
Sansheng asked Linji, "What is the meaning of the First Patriarch's coming from the West?"

Linji said, "If he had any meaning, he could not even save himself."

Sansheng said, "If he had no meaning, how did the Second Patriarch attain the Dharma?"

Linji said, "Attaining is not attaining."

Sansheng said, "If that is so, what is the meaning of your teaching for the past thirty years?"

Linji said, "This blind ass!"
locator: Linji Lu, Sansheng exchange on the blind ass

title: The Golden Fish Through the Net
content:
When Linji was about to pass away, he said, "After I am gone, do not destroy my treasury of the true Dharma eye."

Sansheng stepped forward and said, "How could I dare to destroy the master's treasury of the true Dharma eye?"

Linji said, "If someone asks you about it in the future, what will you say?"

Sansheng gave a great shout.

Linji said, "Who would have thought that my treasury of the true Dharma eye would perish at the hands of this blind ass?"

Sansheng said, "The golden-scaled fish has already passed through the net."
locator: Linji Lu, deathbed exchange with Sansheng

title: Mountain Hermitage
content:
My hut sits among a thousand peaks,
the path overgrown with moss.
Clouds come and go as they please,
and the moon keeps me company at night.
No bell marks the hours,
no visitor breaks the silence.
I sit until the incense burns to ash
and the mountain stream speaks the Dharma.
locator: Shiwu Shanfang Ji, mountain hermitage verses

title: The Great Kalpa Fire
content:
A monk asked Dasui, "When the great kalpa fire blazes and the whole universe is destroyed, is this destroyed or not?"

Dasui said, "Destroyed."

The monk said, "Then it goes along with the other?"

Dasui said, "It goes along with the other."
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Case 29

title: Seeing Through
content: Poan said: "If you can see through just one phrase completely, you see through a thousand phrases, ten thousand phrases, all at once. If you cannot see through one phrase, how will more words help you? Do not seek extensively—turn the light around and look at what is looking."
locator: Wudeng Huiyuan, Poan Zuxian chapter

title: Tianlong Raises One Finger
content:
A monk asked Tianlong, "What is the Buddha?"

Tianlong simply raised one finger.

The monk asked, "What is the Dharma?"

Tianlong raised one finger.

The monk asked, "What is the meaning of the patriarch's coming from the West?"

Tianlong raised one finger.

Whatever he was asked, Tianlong only raised one finger. His student Juzhi saw this and was greatly enlightened, and thereafter he too answered every question by raising one finger.
locator: Wumenguan, Case 3, Tianlong's one-finger teaching

title: The Meaning of Bodhidharma's Coming from the West
content:
A monk asked Xianglin, "What is the meaning of the patriarch's coming from the West?"

Xianglin said, "Sitting for a long time becomes toilsome."
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Case 17

title: The Lotus Flower
content:
A monk asked Zhimen, "What is the lotus flower before it has emerged from the water?"

Zhimen said, "A lotus flower."

The monk asked, "What about after it has emerged from the water?"

Zhimen said, "Lotus leaves."
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Case 21

title: Original Nature
content: Qinshan said: "Your original nature is like a clear mirror. It does not come into being when you polish it, nor does it cease to be when dust covers it. The mirror has never changed—why do you keep trying to improve it?"
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Qinshan Wensui chapter

title: Silent Sitting
content:
A monk asked Jiufeng, "What is the meaning of the master's silent sitting?"

Jiufeng said, "When have I ever sat silently?"

The monk said, "You have been sitting here without speaking all morning."

Jiufeng said, "Not speaking is not the same as silence. The whole universe is speaking right now. Can't you hear it?"
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Jiufeng Qin chapter

title: Nowhere to Stand
content: Baofeng said: "If you take a single step forward, you stumble over your own feet. If you take a single step back, you fall into the pit of concepts. If you stand still, you are a dead stump. Tell me—where will you plant your feet?"
locator: Wudeng Huiyuan, Baofeng Weizhao chapter

title: No Outside Dharma
content: Huguo said: "There is no Dharma outside the mind, and there is no mind outside the Dharma. When you look for the mind, you cannot find it. When you stop looking, it is everywhere. This is not something that can be given or received—each person must verify it for themselves."
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Huguo Shoucheng chapter

title: Verse on Case 1: Bodhidharma's Vast Emptiness
content:
Vast emptiness, nothing holy—
the approach is far too lofty.
Had he known what would come after,
he would have shut his mouth for good.
But the old barbarian sailed across the sea in vain,
and to this day there are those who vainly search for him.
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Xuedou's verse on Case 1

title: Poetry and Zen
content: Xuedou said: "If you want to express the inexpressible, you must use the sword that kills and the sword that gives life. A verse that merely describes is dead words. A verse that cuts through all description and makes you gasp—that is the living phrase. My hundred verses are not literature. Each one is a trap for a dragon."
locator: Xuedou Songgu Ji, preface on versifying koans

title: Verse on Case 6: Yunmen's Every Day Is a Good Day
content:
He throws away one, picks up seven,
above, below, and in the four directions, nothing compares.
Walking slowly, treading the dawn moon,
gazing far into the clouds beyond the blue sky.
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Xuedou's verse on Case 6

title: The Blown Hair Sword
content:
A monk asked Baling, "What is the blown hair sword?"

Baling said, "Each branch of coral holds up the moon."
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Case 100

title: Longya Visits Two Masters
content:
Longya asked Cuiwei, "What is the meaning of the patriarch's coming from the West?"

Cuiwei said, "Pass me that meditation cushion."

As Longya handed it over, Cuiwei took it and hit him with it.

Longya said, "You may hit me as you wish. In the end, there is no meaning of the patriarch's coming from the West."

Later Longya asked Linji the same question.

Linji said, "Pass me that meditation cushion."

As Longya handed it over, Linji took it and hit him.

Longya said, "You may hit me as you wish. In the end, there is no meaning of the patriarch's coming from the West."

Linji said, "That is so."
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Longya Judun chapter

title: Iron Grinder Liu
content:
Liu Tiemo came to see Guishan. Guishan said, "So the old cow has come!"

Liu Tiemo said, "Tomorrow there is a great feast on Mount Tai. Will the master be going?"

Guishan lay down and stretched out.

Liu Tiemo turned and walked out.

A monk later asked Guishan about this exchange. Guishan said, "That old woman—she ground me right down."
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Liu Tiemo chapter

title: The Moon of the Mind
content: Panshan said: "The moon of the mind is solitary and round; its light swallows the ten thousand forms. The light does not illuminate objects, nor do objects exist. Light and objects both forgotten—then what is this?"
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Panshan Baoji chapter

title: Mayu's Fan
content:
Mayu was fanning himself. A monk came and said, "The nature of wind is constant and all-pervading. There is no place it does not reach. Why then do you fan yourself?"

Mayu said, "You understand that the nature of wind is constant, but you do not yet understand the meaning of reaching every place."

The monk said, "What is the meaning of reaching every place?"

Mayu just kept fanning himself.

The monk bowed deeply.
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Mayu Baoche chapter

title: Beating the Drum
content:
A monk asked Heshan, "How can one hear the soundless teaching?"

Heshan said, "When the wooden man beats the drum at midnight, the stone woman gets up and dances."
locator: Wudeng Huiyuan, Heshan Wuyin chapter

title: Meeting the Self
content: Gaoan said: "You have been running around seeking the Buddha outside yourself for countless kalpas. Have you found him? Stop running, sit down, and for the first time you will meet the one who has been searching all along. That one is already complete."
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Gaoan Dayu chapter

title: Before Thinking Arises
content: Xingyang said: "Before a single thought arises, where is the distinction between sacred and profane? In that gap, the whole great earth is the gate of liberation. But the moment you try to dwell there, you have already missed it."
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Xingyang Qingpou chapter

title: Verse on Case 14: Yunmen's Appropriate Statement
content:
On the highest peak, a road goes beyond;
the boundless ocean, the bottom exposed.
Four gates, eight openings, all face the central post—
one phrase transcends all regulations.
locator: Blue Cliff Record, Xuedou's verse on Case 14

title: Tending the Fire
content: Longya said to the assembly: "Those who study the Way must first learn to tend the fire. What fire? The fire of delusion that burns day and night. If you try to extinguish it, you only fan the flames. If you let it burn freely, it consumes itself. When the fuel is spent, what remains?"
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Longya Judun chapter

title: The Three Realms Have No Dharma
content:
Panshan addressed the assembly: "The three realms have no dharma. Where will you seek the mind? The four elements are originally empty. Where does the Buddha dwell? The heavenly truth does not move, perfectly still. Approach and I will reveal it to you."

A monk stepped forward and bowed.

Panshan said, "Too late."
locator: Jingde Chuandeng Lu, Panshan Baoji dharma hall discourse

title: A Silver Bowl Filled with Snow
content:
A monk asked Baling, "What is the school of Yunmen?"

Baling said, "A silver bowl filled with snow."
locator: Wudeng Huiyuan, Baling Haojian chapter
